2026-07-23T21:10:39.7105568Z ##[group]Run # Replace (not delete) non-alphanumeric characters so stripped punctuation never welds
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7106276Z [36;1m# Replace (not delete) non-alphanumeric characters so stripped punctuation never welds[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7106885Z [36;1m# two words together (e.g. "rsksmart/rit-update" must not become "rsksmartrit-update"),[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7107738Z [36;1m# then squeeze runs of spaces and trim, and cap length to keep the Slack message concise.[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7108168Z [36;1msanitize() {[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7108517Z [36;1m  printf '%s' "$1" | tr -c '[:alnum:]_ -' ' ' | tr -s ' ' | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//' | cut -c1-75[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7108897Z [36;1m}[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7109076Z [36;1m[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7109403Z [36;1m# Write NAME=VALUE to GITHUB_ENV as a heredoc with a random delimiter, so a crafted[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7109995Z [36;1m# value (e.g. a PR title containing newlines) can never inject additional variables.[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7110403Z [36;1mwrite_env() {[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7110710Z [36;1m  _delim="__EOF_$(od -An -N16 -tx1 /dev/urandom | tr -d ' \n')__"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7111037Z [36;1m  {[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7111230Z [36;1m    echo "$1<<$_delim"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7111455Z [36;1m    echo "$2"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7111659Z [36;1m    echo "$_delim"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7111878Z [36;1m  } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7112089Z [36;1m}[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7112259Z [36;1m[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7112484Z [36;1mif [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = pull_request ]; then[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7112879Z [36;1m  NOTIFICATION_TITLE="PR #${GITHUB_EVENT_PULL_REQUEST_NUMBER}"[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7113358Z [36;1m  NOTIFICATION_SUBTITLE=$(sanitize "$GITHUB_EVENT_PULL_REQUEST_TITLE")[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7113932Z [36;1m  # A PR title made up entirely of characters sanitize() replaces with spaces (e.g. only emoji)[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7114519Z [36;1m  # would otherwise produce an empty string, which Slack's Block Kit rejects (text[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7115064Z [36;1m  # objects require a minimum length of 1), failing this step and misreporting a[0m
2026-07-23T21:10:39.7115474Z [36;1m  # successful run as failed.[0m
